ground_truth.title: Best Practices for PTO Benefits
ground_truth.main_content:
One of the best ways to maintain a high level of employee engagement and retainment is by offering a competitive benefits package. Furthermore, having a strong company paid time off policy is key to ensure that your company remains highly regarded in the eyes of current employees and potential applicants. There are many things to consider when planning for and managing paid time off benefits for your employees.

Choose which types and how much PTO you would like to offer. The first step to offering a great paid time off policy for your company is to do research. Researching industry standards for the categories and amount of paid time off that competitors offer gives you an idea of how your PTO policy should look. Being competitive on this benefit will make your company attractive to potential applicants. Types of paid time off to consider include sick time, vacation time, holidays, personal days, and paid leave.

Decide on how and when you will distribute PTO. Options include Lump Sum PTO (providing the full allotment of time off at one time), Accrued PTO (allowing employees to earn time off over the course of the year), and Unlimited PTO (giving an unlimited amount of time off to employees). You will also need to decide if you will offer PTO to all employees or only full-time employees, and if employees receive time off immediately upon hire or after a certain waiting period.

Think about how you will allow your employees to request and use their PTO. Having proper steps in place for employees to use their time off is essential. Think about your business' needs and create policies beforehand and be consistent in applying these policies. You will need to establish how employees should request time off, who they request with, who approves requests, and how scheduling coverage is handled.

Plan on how you will keep track of PTO balances. The best way to manage PTO is by using a time and attendance software that allows you to easily manage and track time and time off. A fully integrated solution allows you to add personalized rules for your business' policies, build customized restrictions around time off requests, allow employees to see their PTO balance and submit requests, allow managers to approve requests, provide insights for scheduling, view customized reports, and renew time off balances.

Create policies on PTO rollovers, pay outs, and more. Creating policies that work for your business and sticking to these policies is key to running a smooth PTO program. You need to have steps in place for when employees ask questions. Know if you are going to allow employees to rollover any paid time off into the new year, and whether employees will be eligible to receive payouts for any time off balances when they leave the company.

Types of paid time off to consider include sick time, vacation time, holidays, personal days, and paid leave.

Lump Sum PTO provides the full allotment of time off to employees at one time, Accrued PTO allows employees to earn time off over the course of the year, and Unlimited PTO gives an unlimited amount of time off.
The best way to manage PTO is by using a time and attendance software that allows you to easily manage and track time and time off.
Creating policies that work for your business and sticking to these policies is key to running a smooth PTO program.
